response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong staff-level engineer with solid distributed-systems fundamentals and broad polyglot experience, but he is a poor fit for this CTO role. The position requires executive leadership of 30+ person engineering organizations, board-level roadmap communication, fintech regulatory expertise, and proven production multi-agent AI architecture. None of these appear substantively in his background. His only AI/agentic exposure is a single line from a role that started in April 2025 (MCP tooling and agentic workflow support), and there is no evidence of people management at scale, governance of hybrid engineering models, or P&L ownership. His career trajectory is that of a high-performing individual contributor, not an executive.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems and high-throughput backend experience (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O with DLang, real-time metadata pipelines)
Demonstrated event-driven and microservices architecture via open-source GISCollective platform, showing autonomous system design capability
Broad polyglot fluency (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, C/C++, Java) and full-stack range enabling architectural breadth across layers
concerns: No executive or large-team engineering leadership — all titles are Staff/Senior IC with zero evidence of managing organizations of 10+ people or developing a senior leadership layer

Negligible agentic AI production depth — the only mention is a brief line in his current role started April 2025, far below the required proven multi-agent system design and operation at production scale
No fintech infrastructure, credit-risk systems, regulatory compliance, or high-stakes financial AI experience — the role's core domain is entirely absent from the resume

key_factors.factor: Strong distributed-systems and real-time backend fundamentals
